summary.title: Lobster / OpenClaw Personal AI Agent
summary.description: STRIDE threat model of the Lobster personal AI agent system built on the OpenClaw framework. Six agents on a dedicated Mac handle iMessage, WhatsApp, email, calendar, HomeKit, and travel. Architecture documented at lobster.shahine.com.

diagrams.title: Lobster OpenClaw DFD
diagrams.placeholder: Lobster / OpenClaw STRIDE Data Flow Diagram

threats.title: Sender spoofing via BlueBubbles
threats.description: BlueBubbles reports phone numbers without cryptographic verification. Compromised API injects messages as owner, routing to main agent with full privileges.
threats.mitigation: Bind BlueBubbles to localhost. Strong API password. Challenge-response verification.

threats.title: BlueBubbles compromise chains to main agent
threats.description: Compromised BlueBubbles sends messages as owner, routing to main agent with full host access, exec, Fastmail.
threats.mitigation: Out-of-band verification for high-impact commands. Anomaly detection for unusual patterns.

threats.title: Message tampering in BlueBubbles bridge
threats.description: HTTP API between iMessage and gateway modifiable without TLS enforcement.
threats.mitigation: Bind to localhost. Tailscale encrypted transport.

threats.title: BlueBubbles crash halts all iMessage
threats.description: Single point of failure for entire iMessage channel.
threats.mitigation: Health monitoring and self-healing scripts. No automatic failover exists.

threats.title: Prompt injection as owner instructions
threats.description: Email bodies containing injection payloads processed in LLM context. Main agent has full exec and Fastmail access to comply.
threats.mitigation: Deterministic content filtering before LLM. Mandatory human-in-the-loop for destructive actions.

threats.title: Email prompt injection triggers privileged actions
threats.description: Crafted email triggers main agent with full Fastmail access to send, archive, forward, and create filters.
threats.mitigation: Mandatory confirmation for all email-modifying actions. Sandboxed email body processing.

threats.title: Spoofed sender bypasses email triage
threats.description: Forged From headers bypass importance classification. DKIM/SPF/DMARC not automatically invoked.
threats.mitigation: Auto-invoke mail-auth-check before processing. System prompt for unknown sender verification.

threats.title: File reads via exec utilities
threats.description: cat, ls, grep in WhatsApp agent exec allowlist bypass workspace isolation for any world-readable file.
threats.mitigation: Remove general utilities from allowlist. Purpose-built read-only tools.

threats.title: WA agent utilities enable arbitrary reads
threats.description: WhatsApp exec allowlist includes cat ls grep head tail for arbitrary file access.
threats.mitigation: chmod 600/700 on sensitive directories.

threats.title: allowUnsafeExternalContent disables injection defense
threats.description: Setting this flag removes the safety wrapper marking webhook payloads as untrusted data. Direct prompt injection.
threats.mitigation: Audit all webhook mappings. Consider removing the flag entirely.

threats.title: Webhook source spoofing
threats.description: Weak or leaked tokens allow arbitrary event injection at webhook endpoints.
threats.mitigation: Strong per-hook tokens. Token rotation.

threats.title: Webhook injection escalates to main agent
threats.description: allowUnsafeExternalContent plus sessions_send chains webhook injection to full-privilege access.
threats.mitigation: Default safety wrapper. Minimal webhook agent tools. Transform filtering.

threats.title: Default fallback grants maximum privileges
threats.description: Without explicit bindings, messages route to full-access main agent.
threats.mitigation: Verify binding coverage for all channels. Invert-the-catch-all strategy.

threats.title: A2A soft privilege escalation
threats.description: Restricted agents send arbitrary requests to main agent via sessions_send. LLM may comply despite privacy rules.
threats.mitigation: Content filtering on A2A messages. Rate limiting. Log and alert on sensitive data requests.

threats.title: Main agent leaks private data via A2A
threats.description: Restricted agent asks for private email. Main agent may comply despite TOOLS.md privacy instructions.
threats.mitigation: TOOLS.md privacy rules (soft). Technical filtering on A2A response content.

threats.title: Private email via A2A escalation
threats.description: Restricted agents ask main agent to read or forward emails via sessions_send.
threats.mitigation: No technical enforcement on response content. Provenance tagging only.

threats.title: Gateway config self-modification
threats.description: If write/edit tools not denied, main agent can modify openclaw.json to escalate its own privileges.
threats.mitigation: Verify write/edit/apply_patch denied for ALL agents. Filesystem immutability on config files.

threats.title: Exec argument injection
threats.description: Allowlist patterns match paths not arguments. Shell metacharacters could chain unauthorized commands.
threats.mitigation: Argument validation at exec engine level. Enforce shell=false. AppArmor profiles per CLI tool.

threats.title: Full filesystem read enables exfiltration
threats.description: Main agent fs.workspaceOnly: false grants read to entire macOS filesystem as AGENT_USER.
threats.mitigation: Set fs.workspaceOnly: true. Grant specific directory access.

threats.title: Main agent compromise = full host compromise
threats.description: Maximum privilege agent. Any successful attack yields full host access, email, calendars, shell execution.
threats.mitigation: Fundamental architectural risk. Reduce privileges where possible. Enhanced monitoring.

threats.title: Symlink traversal bypasses exec allowlist
threats.description: Wildcards in allowlist may be bypassed via symlinks or path traversal.
threats.mitigation: Verify exec engine resolves symlinks. Add realpath() validation.

threats.title: Agent self-modification risk
threats.description: Architecture doc shows write/edit in alsoAllow. If deployed, agent can rewrite own prompts and skills.
threats.mitigation: Verify actual deployed config matches hardening guide.

threats.title: Dual config security drift
threats.description: openclaw.json and exec-approvals.json must both be correct. Changes to one silently degrade security.
threats.mitigation: Automated consistency validation. Startup checks. Alert on drift.

threats.title: Shared API keys across all agents
threats.description: All six agents share same API key. Compromise of any agent workspace yields all credentials.
threats.mitigation: Per-agent API key isolation. Separate keys per agent tier.

threats.title: jq resets file permissions
threats.description: Config edits via jq write to /tmp then mv, resetting chmod. Files world-readable until manually fixed.
threats.mitigation: Wrapper script restoring chmod 600 after jq. inotify monitoring.

threats.title: Gateway crash stops all agents
threats.description: Single gateway process hosts all six agents. Crash stops all channels simultaneously.
threats.mitigation: launchd process supervision with automatic restart. Health check endpoints.

threats.title: Unrestricted outbound exfiltration
threats.description: Agent makes arbitrary outbound connections. No egress filtering documented.
threats.mitigation: Egress filtering with domain allowlisting. Monitoring proxy.

threats.title: Owner mention in group elevates restricted agent
threats.description: Group participants can manipulate conversation context before owner @mention triggers elevation.
threats.mitigation: Remove elevation from group chats. Require DM confirmation.
